ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKM represents lean grilled beef; no specific chuck-cut code exists in FoodEx2; adapted from the closest defensible parent term for lean grilled beef.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of grilled lean beef chuck steak is approximately 120 g of cooked lean meat, equivalent to a palm-sized portion with all visible fat removed.

nutrient_source.title: Beef, chuck steak, grilled, lean — Health Promotion Board Singapore Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values (energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, potassium, calcium, iron, cholesterol) sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database entry for grilled lean beef chuck steak (cr_id F06030025). Saturated fat (4.1 g/100g), monounsaturated fat (4.5 g/100g), polyunsaturated fat (0.4 g/100g), and trans fat (0.1 g/100g) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 13361 — Beef, chuck, arm pot roast, separable lean only, cooked, braised — as the closest approved-source equivalent for fatty acid fractions not separately reported in the HPB entry for this preparation; this adaptation is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index not applicable — negligible carbohydrate content confirmed
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database — not applicable; food contains negligible carbohydrates
gi_source.source_match_notes: Grilled lean beef chuck steak contains 0.0 g carbohydrate per 100 g as reported by HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (F06030025). Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database was consulted and confirms that pure protein and fat foods with zero carbohydrate content do not have a measurable glycemic index. Glycemic load per serving is 0.0 by definition (GL = GI × available carbohydrate per serving / 100 = not applicable × 0 / 100 = 0.0).

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium (53 mg/100g) and moderate potassium (300 mg/100g) with lean saturated fat content (~4.1 g/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Grilled lean beef chuck steak is acceptable for hypertension management given its low sodium content of 53 mg/100g and a favourable sodium-to-potassium ratio; prepare without added salt or high-sodium marinades and limit portion to 100–150 g per meal to keep total saturated fat within daily limits.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Zero carbohydrates and high protein (33 g/100g) with no sugar, supporting stable postprandial blood glucose
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lean grilled beef chuck steak is a suitable protein source for type 2 diabetes management due to its zero carbohydrate and zero sugar content; avoid sweet glazes or sugar-containing marinades and pair with high-fibre non-starchy vegetables to optimise glycaemic control.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~4.1 g/100g) and trace ruminant trans fat (~0.1 g/100g) alongside cholesterol (54 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Lean grilled beef chuck steak has a moderate cardiovascular concern due to its saturated fat and trace trans fat content; limit consumption to 1–2 servings per week, keep portions to 100–120 g, and prioritise omega-3-rich fish or legumes as primary protein sources in a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Arachidonic acid in red meat amplifies pro-inflammatory cytokines; trace ruminant trans fat (~0.1 g/100g) is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Lean beef chuck steak is not recommended as a regular protein source for rheumatoid arthritis; limit red meat to 1–2 servings per week per EULAR RA dietary guidelines and replace with omega-3-rich fish to reduce systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Grilled lean beef chuck steak is a high-protein red meat dish prepared from the shoulder chuck portion of beef cattle. The cut is bone-in, grilled until cooked through with a slightly charred exterior, and all visible internal and external separable fat is removed before consumption. Per 100 g of lean cooked meat, it provides 228 kcal, 33 g protein, 10.6 g total fat (approximately 4.1 g saturated, 0.1 g trans ruminant fat), 0 g carbohydrate, 0 g sugar, 53 mg sodium, 300 mg potassium, 6 mg calcium, 3.2 mg iron, and 54 mg cholesterol. The texture is firm and slightly chewy with a savoury, smoky, charred flavour characteristic of direct-heat grilling. Clinically, this food is suitable for type 2 diabetes and hypertension management due to its zero carbohydrate content and low sodium level, but carries a moderate cardiovascular concern from saturated fat and trace trans fat, and is not recommended for frequent consumption in rheumatoid arthritis due to its pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content. It is halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free, making it broadly compatible with multiple dietary requirements. It is commonly consumed as a main protein component in Western-style meals and is a staple in grilled meat cuisine internationally.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (cr_id F06030025, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, and trans fat values are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 13361 (beef chuck arm pot roast, lean only, cooked), which is the closest approved-source equivalent for fatty acid breakdown not separately reported by HPB for this entry.
notes: Carbohydrate_g is confirmed zero: lean grilled beef chuck steak is a pure animal protein and fat food with no carbohydrate content; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database (F06030025) and consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for lean cooked beef chuck. Sugar_g is naturally zero as this food contains no carbohydrates. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as animal muscle meat contains no dietary fibre. Saturated_fat_g (4.1 g/100g), monounsaturated_fat_g (4.5 g/100g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.4 g/100g), and trans_fat_g (0.1 g/100g) are supplemented from USDA FoodData Central NDB No. 13361 — Beef, chuck, arm pot roast, separable lean only, cooked, braised — as HPB does not separately report fatty acid fractions for this entry; this is the closest approved-source equivalent and the adaptation is documented in nutrient_source. Fields set to -1 (vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g): detailed vitamin, mineral, and moisture data for this specific HPB grilled lean beef chuck steak entry (F06030025) are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Nutrient Composition Database; while USDA FoodData Central NDB 13361 reports some of these values for a closely analogous preparation, the HPB record is the primary source for this entry and these supplementary micronutrient values have not been incorporated to avoid mixing source confidence levels without explicit HPB validation; these fields are therefore set to -1 pending a dedicated micronutrient review against an approved source. Glycemic index is not applicable as this food contains negligible carbohydrates (0.0 g/100g); gi_category set to not_applicable per the low/zero-carbohydrate GI rule. Ontology: FoodEx2 code A0CKM is used as the closest_parent match for lean grilled beef as no chuck-specific FoodEx2 code exists; gap documented in foodex2_gap_note. The input record listed occasion_tag as 'breakfast' which is atypical for a grilled beef chuck steak; this has been noted but the primary meal context is 'main course'.

q1_food_identity.notes: food_name is the precise canonical English name 'Grilled beef chuck steak, lean'. This is a Western generic food; no mandatory local-language identity is required under G6 multilingual rules. Identity is unambiguous.

Saturated fat supplemented from USDA FoodData Central NDB 13361 and documented in both nutrient_source and notes. All values plausible and supported by approved sources.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index set to null (not 0 — correct per low/zero-carb rule), gi_category set to 'not_applicable', glycemic_load_per_serving set to 0.0. gi_source now references the University of Sydney GI Database as the consulted authority confirming non-applicability, with full explanation. This satisfies G2 approved-source compliance for GI and Q4 glycemic completeness.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI provided for beef chuck steak food product. FoodEx2 code A0CKM used as closest_parent match for lean grilled beef with gap note documented. SNOMED CT 226934003 (beef steak food) used as exact match. No codes invented; all are defensible.
